title: [lichen_l2.c:1549] rx_link_key copied but original may be modified concurrently
description: At line 1549, rx_link_key is copied from link_ctx.link_key while holding rx_mutex. However, the comment says this is to protect against 'future refactor' that moves cleanup outside rx_mutex. The current code holds rx_mutex for the entire lichen_l2_input(), so this is safe. But note that the peer_try_all_pubkeys call at line 1589 releases rx_mutex? No - actually rx_mutex is held continuously. The function is correct but the defensive copy suggests concern about a race that cannot currently occur. The issue is more that link_ctx.link_key could be modified by lichen_l2_enable(disable=true) but that path also acquires rx_mutex, so no race.
close_reason: Fixed: Added issue reference to existing comment explaining the defensive copy is intentional for future-proofing
